source_jobs.title: Recruiter & Talent Operations Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Recruiter & Talent Operations Coordinator

Company: Knowledge Road Education
Department: Human Resources
Employment Type: Full-time
Compensation: $20–25/hr + commission and performance bonuses

About Knowledge Road Education

Knowledge Road Education provides specialized educational programs, therapy services, and EdTech solutions to schools and families. We focus on delivering high-quality educational experiences that improve student outcomes through innovative approaches and dedicated professionals.



Position Overview

We're seeking a Recruiter & Talent Operations Coordinator to lead our hiring efforts across educational roles including teachers, therapists, administrators, and support staff. This role requires experience in education sector recruiting, strong relationship-building skills, and the ability to identify candidates who align with our mission of student success.



Key Responsibilities

- Source and recruit qualified educators, therapists, and administrative staff

- Manage full-cycle recruiting process from initial outreach through offer negotiation

- Build and maintain talent pipeline using applicant tracking systems

- Conduct initial candidate screenings and coordinate interview processes

- Partner with hiring managers to understand role requirements and team dynamics

- Develop recruiting strategies for hard-to-fill positions

- Maintain relationships with educational institutions and professional networks - Support onboarding process for new hires



Required Qualifications

- 2+ years of recruiting experience, preferably in education or healthcare

- Understanding of educational roles, certifications, and licensing requirements

- Experience with applicant tracking systems and recruiting tools - Strong interviewing and candidate assessment skills

- Excellent communication and relationship-building abilities

- Knowledge of employment law and hiring best practices

- Self-motivated with ability to manage multiple searches simultaneously



Preferred Qualifications

- Background in education or experience recruiting for schools/districts

- Familiarity with special education and therapy roles

- Experience with diversity, equity, and inclusion in hiring

- Knowledge of teacher certification and licensing processes

- Existing network of education professionals

- Experience with LinkedIn Recruiter and other sourcing tools



Success Metrics

- Fill 3-5 open positions within first 90 days

- Maintain average time-to-fill of 30 days or less

- Achieve 90%+ hiring manager satisfaction scores



Compensation & Benefits

- Base salary: $20–25/hr based on experience

- Performance bonuses tied to successful placements and retention

- Daily catered lunch

- Company laptop and voip phone

- Generous PTO policy

- Professional development budget

- Potential equity participation



How to Apply

Submit your resume along with examples of successful hires you've made, particularly in education or related fields. Include metrics showing your recruiting performance and any relevant certifications.

---

Knowledge Road Education is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity in education.

Knowledge Road Education is dedicated to supporting schools and families by providing high-quality special education and therapy services. We specialize in SETSS (Special Education Teacher Support Services), Speech Therapy, Occupational Therapy, Counseling, and ABA therapy through insurance partnerships. Our team values high performance, accountability, and measurable results, ensuring that more students gain access to the services they need.

Position Overview
We're looking for a Growth Marketing Specialist to manage paid advertising campaigns and optimize conversion funnels across multiple platforms. This role requires hands-on experience with digital advertising, strong analytical skills, and a track record of improving campaign ROI through testing and optimization.

Key Responsibilities

source_jobs.title: School & District Partnerships Lead
source_jobs.description:
SCHOOL & DISTRICT PARTNERSHIPS LEAD

Company: Knowledge Road Education
Department: Accounts & Business Development
Employment Type: Full-time
Compensation: $20–30/hr + commission opportunities


ABOUT KNOWLEDGE ROAD EDUCATION

Knowledge Road Education is dedicated to providing high-quality therapy and educational support services to schools and families throughout our community. We specialize in SETSS (Special Education Teacher Support Services), Speech Therapy, Occupational Therapy, and Counseling services. Additionally, we offer ABA therapy through insurance partnerships to support families in need of these essential services.




POSITION OVERVIEW

We’re seeking a School & District Partnerships Lead who thrives at the intersection of customer success and business development. In this role, you’ll build lasting relationships with schools and districts while ensuring current partners are supported, engaged, and satisfied.



Your time will be split between:

 - Supporting existing school partners through regular check-ins (virtual and in-person), ensuring families receive the services they need, and providing exceptional customer service.

 - Driving new growth opportunities by introducing Knowledge Road’s services to new schools, districts, and referral partners.



This is a field-based position with flexibility and independence. You’ll visit schools, host virtual check-ins, attend meetings, and connect with education professionals who share our commitment to student success.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Partnership Management & Client Success

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for assigned schools and districts.

 - Conduct regular in-person and virtual check-ins with school administrators, special education teams, and families.

 - Troubleshoot issues and ensure high satisfaction with Knowledge Road’s services.

 - Provide proactive communication, resources, and solutions to support school needs.



School & District Partnership Development

 - Introduce Knowledge Road’s SETSS, Speech Therapy, OT, Counseling, and ABA services to new schools and districts.

 - Conduct outreach calls, schedule presentations, and facilitate both virtual and in-person meetings.

 - Build trust with administrators and staff to expand partnerships.

 - Maintain accurate pipeline records in the company CRM, ensuring clear tracking and follow-up.



Family & Insurance Coordination

 - Support families in navigating insurance coverage for ABA therapy services.

 - Collaborate with insurance providers to facilitate approvals.

 - Build relationships with healthcare providers and community partners.

 - Ensure compliance and documentation accuracy for insurance processes.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Required Qualifications

 - 2+ years in business-to-business sales, client success, or relationship management.

 - Strong communication and presentation skills across in-person and virtual settings.

 - Experience balancing both customer support and new business development.

 - Understanding of educational environments and special education services.

 - Reliable transportation and comfort with regular in-person client visits.

 - Familiarity with insurance or healthcare coordination.



Preferred Qualifications

 - Previous experience working with schools, healthcare, or social services.

 - Knowledge of special education regulations and therapy services.

 - Existing professional network in education or healthcare.

 - Experience with insurance billing or Medicaid processes.

 - Background in ABA therapy or developmental services.




SUCCESS METRICS

 - Establish productive relationships with 5+ new schools within the first 90 days.

 - Maintain satisfaction and strong engagement with all assigned school partners.

 - Develop partnerships with 3+ insurance providers within 6 months.

 - Maintain an active pipeline of 50+ prospective partners and referral sources.

 - Achieve quarterly goals for new referrals, renewals, and revenue growth.




CO

Special Education Teacher (SETSS) – Manhattan, NY – During School – Full-Time



Current Openings:

We are currently seeking candidates for the following full-time SETSS positions in the Manhattan area:

Location: New York, NY 10010

Schedule:
Monday–Friday | 8:30 AM – 2:45 PM

Student Population:
Grades 4 - 8



Overview/Impact:

Are you a passionate Special Education Teacher ready to make a significant impact on students' academic development in the Brooklyn area? Knowledge Road is looking for a full-time Special Education Teacher (SETSS) to join our team and provide critical support for students in NYC Department of Education (DOE) schools. With a substantial shortage city-wide of qualified special education teachers, your skills are urgently needed to help students get the individualized instruction they need. This is an opportunity to work directly with students who require specialized academic support, helping them reach their learning goals through targeted intervention and evidence-based teaching.



As a SETSS teacher with Knowledge Road, you will provide individualized and small-group instruction in educational settings, working closely with families and school teams to promote student progress and academic success.



Responsibilities:

As a Special Education Teacher (SETSS) with Knowledge Road, you will:

 - Provide high-quality, individualized, and small-group instruction for students with disabilities, as outlined in their Individualized Education Service Programs (IESP).
   
   

 - Ensure completion of each individual student's weekly IESP requirements and keep an intervention schedule aligned with the school schedule.
   
   

 - Develop and submit quarterly progress reports and maintain regular family communication with updates on progress and next steps.
   
   

 - Document service delivery and student performance in Knowledge Road's system with precise and timely documentation.
   
   
   

 - Create and implement engaging and effective lesson plans that meet the unique academic, social, and emotional needs of each individual student.
   
   

 - Collaborate with parents, teachers, and other school staff to build a supportive and inclusive learning environment for students.
   
   

 - Track and evaluate student progress, providing regular updates to families and school-based teams.
   
   

 - Attend IESP meetings as required, providing professional insights and recommendations to support
   
   

Qualifications:

• Valid NYS Students with Disabilities (1-6, 7-12, or All Grades).

• Minimum of 1 year of relevant experience.

• Must not have been employed by NYC DOE in the past 12 months.

• Dependable transportation.

• NYC DOE fingerprinting clearance required, or willingness to obtain prior to start.







Schedule & Location:

This is a full-time, school-day role with a schedule aligned with the NYC DOE calendar. Services will be delivered on-site at a school in the Brooklyn area, with placements based on student needs. Weekly hours are determined by IESP mandates; caseload may fluctuate by placement. Typically 20~30 hours/week , depending on caseload, with extra cases and hours available through coordination with our recruitment team.



Compensation:

$60 - $100 per hour (1099). Rates vary by group size and placement. Individual IESP sessions generally start at $60/hr.



Why Join Knowledge Road?

At Knowledge Road, you will benefit from:

 - 1099 contractor position with prompt, dependable payments

 - Efficient administrative and documentation processes

 - Helpful team providing DOE compliance and scheduling assistance

 - Professional community of NYC school-based practitioners



EEO Statement

Knowledge Road is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

source_jobs.title: NYS-Certified Special Education Teacher — High School ICT (Grades 11–12)
source_jobs.description:
Role: NYS-Certified Special Education Teacher — High School ICT (Grades 11–12)
Location: Downtown Manhattan (10004)
Type: Full-Time Contract | W-2 or 1099 (your choice)
Schedule: Monday–Friday | 9:00 AM–3:30 PM (partial schedules considered)
Pay: $55/hour
Start: Immediate | Renewable year after year


WHO WE ARE

Knowledge Road is a special education staffing company that partners with schools to place strong, reliable educators — and actually supports them once they’re placed. Our goal is straightforward: help students with IEPs access grade-level learning and make real progress, with a team culture that respects teachers.


THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re hiring a NYS-certified Special Education Teacher for a High School Integrated Co-Teaching (ICT) assignment supporting 11th and 12th grade students at a school in downtown Manhattan. This is a push-in / inclusion role across multiple classrooms, working in close partnership with general education teachers across core subjects.

You’ll support instruction in ELA, Math, History, and Science, helping students with IEPs succeed in rigorous, credit-bearing coursework.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Co-teach and co-plan with general education teachers using the ICT model

 - Push in to multiple classrooms to provide accommodations, scaffolds, and targeted support

 - Work 1:1 and in small groups to reinforce skills, improve access, and build independence

 - Implement IEPs with fidelity: accommodations, modifications, goal support, and progress tracking

 - Help adjust assignments, assessments, and materials to meet diverse learning needs

 - Communicate clearly with school staff and families as needed (professional, consistent, calm)


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - NYS certification required: Students with Disabilities (Grades 7–12)

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s in Special Education

 - Experience in ICT / inclusion / collaborative teaching strongly preferred

 - Strong IEP knowledge (instructional + compliance)

 - Organized, flexible, and able to manage a schedule across multiple classrooms

 - Team-first mindset — you collaborate well and don’t do the lone-wolf thing


WHAT YOU GET

 - $55/hour + consistent bi-weekly pay

 - W-2 or 1099 flexibility depending on your preference

 - Long-term stability: renewable assignment year after year

 - Ongoing support from Knowledge Road (real humans, not a ghosted inbox)

 - Access to professional development + educator resources

 - Predictable school-day schedule

Please Apply now — we move fast for qualified candidates.

source_jobs.title: Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW)
source_jobs.description:
Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) - Manhattan, NY - Full-Time - School Day



Overview/Impact

Are you a dedicated Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) passionate about supporting the mental health and academic achievement of students in Manhattan?



Knowledge Road is seeking a full-time Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) to join our team and provide critical psychological services to students in NYC Department of Education (DOE) schools. With an unprecedented need for mental health support in schools and a significant shortage of qualified professionals, your skills are urgently needed to help students navigate complex emotional and behavioral challenges. This is an opportunity to make a meaningful impact on the lives of children and adolescents through evidence-based psychological interventions.



As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) with Knowledge Road, you will have the autonomy to deliver individualized interventions, working collaboratively with school teams and families to support student mental health and academic achievement.



Responsibilities

As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) with Knowledge Road, you will:

 - Provide direct, high-quality individual and group counseling services to students, as mandated by their Individualized Education Service Programs/Individualized Education Programs (IESP/IEP).
   
   

 - Address a comprehensive range of social, emotional, and behavioral concerns, including anxiety, depression, trauma, ADHD, autism spectrum disorders, and complex psychological conditions.
   
   

 - Conduct psychological assessments and evaluations to inform treatment planning and educational recommendations.
   
   

 - Develop and implement evidence-based treatment plans utilizing various therapeutic modalities tailored to the unique developmental and cultural needs of each student.
   
   

 - Collaborate closely with parents, teachers, school administrators, and other mental health professionals to create a holistic and supportive learning environment.
   
   

 - Maintain meticulous and timely documentation of all services, progress, and outcomes in accordance with DOE regulations, HIPAA requirements, and Knowledge Road standards.
   
   

 - Actively participate in IESP/IEP meetings, offering expert psychological insights and recommendations to promote student progress and educational planning.



Qualifications

 - NYS license as a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW).

 - Master's degree in Social Work from a CSWE-accredited program.

 - Minimum of 2 years of experience providing psychological services for children and adolescents.

 - Proficiency with electronic documentation systems and teletherapy platforms.

 - NYC DOE fingerprinting and vendor status required or willingness to obtain prior to start.



Preferred :

 - Experience in a school-based setting or educational environment.

 - Specialized training in child and adolescent therapy modalities (e.g., CBT, DBT, play therapy, family therapy).

 - Experience with psychological assessment and testing.

As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) with Knowledge Road, you will:

 - Provide direct, high-quality individual and group counseling services to students, as mandated by their Individualized Education Service Programs/Individualized Education Programs (IESP/IEP).
   
   

 - Address a comprehensive range of social, emotional, and behavioral concerns, including anxiety, depression, trauma, ADHD, autism spectrum disorders, and complex psychological conditions.
   
   

 - Conduct psychological assessments and evaluations to inform treatment planning and educational recommendations.
   
   

 - Develop and implement evidence-based treatment plans utilizing various therapeutic modalities tailored to the unique developmental and cultural needs of each student.
   
   

 - Collaborate closely with parents, teachers, school administrators, and other mental health professionals to create a holistic and supportive learning environment.
   
   

 - Maintain meticulous and timely documentation of all services, progress, and outcomes in accordance with DOE regulations, HIPAA requirements, and Knowledge Road standards.
   
   

 - Actively participate in IESP/IEP meetings, offering expert psychological insights and recommendations to promote student progress and educational planning.

 - NYS license as a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW).

 - Master's degree in Social Work from a CSWE-accredited program.

 - Minimum of 2 years of experience providing psychological services for children and adolescents.

source_jobs.title: Occupational Therapist (OT)
source_jobs.description:
OCCUPATIONAL THERAPIST (OT) – BRONX, NY – SCHOOL DAY – FULL-TIME



Overview/Impact:



Are you an experienced and passionate Occupational Therapist dedicated to helping students in the Bronx build the critical skills needed to succeed academically and independently? We are seeking a full-time Occupational Therapist (OT) to join our team and deliver essential support for students in NYC Department of Education (DOE) schools. With a major shortage city-wide of related services providers, your skills are urgently needed to help students develop essential life skills and achieve their goals. This is a chance to make a meaningful impact in the lives of students with varied needs using evidence-based occupational therapy approaches.



As an OT with Knowledge Road, you will have the flexibility to deliver personalized therapy interventions, working with school teams and families to promote student growth and independence.



Responsibilities

As an Occupational Therapist (OT) with Knowledge Road, you will:

 - Provide high-quality occupational therapy services for students in individual and group settings, as mandated by their Individualized Education Service Programs/Individualized Education Programs (IESP/IEP).
   
   

 - Conduct thorough assessments to evaluate students' fine motor, visual-motor, sensory processing, and daily living skills.
   
   

 - Create and execute evidence-based treatment plans utilizing sensory integration, motor learning principles, and adaptive strategies.
   
   

 - Adjust and modify classroom environments, learning materials, and assistive devices to improve student participation and independence.
   
   

 - Work closely with parents, teachers, related service providers, and school administrators to ensure coordinated care and optimal outcomes.
   
   

 - Offer guidance and training to educational staff on positioning, adaptive equipment usage, and sensory strategies.
   
   

 - Detailed and timely documentation of all services, progress, and outcomes per DOE regulations and Knowledge Road standards.
   
   

 - Participate actively in IESP/IEP meetings, providing expert occupational therapy insights and evidence-based recommendations.
   
   

Qualifications

• Current valid NYS license as an Occupational Therapist in good standing.

• NBCOT (National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy) certification.

• Minimum of 1-2 years of experience in school-based or pediatric settings.

• Competency in electronic documentation systems and teletherapy platforms.

• NYC DOE fingerprinting and vendor status required or willingness to complete before start.



Schedule & Location:

This full-time, school-day position follows a school day schedule aligned with the NYC DOE calendar. You will provide services on-site at schools in the Bronx area, with placements based on student needs. Hours per week are determined by IESP/IEP mandates; caseload may vary by assignment. Generally ~20-30 hours/week , depending on placements.



Compensation

$63/hr (1099).



Why Join Knowledge Road?

At Knowledge Road, you will benefit from:

• 1099 contractor position with quick, dependable payments

• Streamlined administrative and documentation processes

• Supportive team providing DOE compliance and scheduling assistance

• Professional community of NYC school-based practitioners



EEO Statement

Knowledge Road is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. We celebrate diverse perspectives and are dedicated to building an inclusive environment for all contractors. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, veteran status.
